make tenex-edge who include the host where the agent is running (even if its the same host)

Done. `tenex-edge who` now includes the host for every agent.

## What changed

The host was previously suppressed for same-machine agents (an old §8e decision to reduce clutter) — only genuine cross-machine peers got a `(remote)` tag. Now every row shows its host:

- **Same machine** → `(laptop)`
- **Different machine** → `(tenex kind2, remote)` — hostname plus a `, remote` flag so cross-machine sessions stay distinguishable

This applies to both renderers in `src/cli.rs`:
- `render_who_row` — the rich `tenex-edge who` CLI output
- `render_who_plain` — the turn-start "tenex-edge fabric" context block injected into agents

The `host` field was already present on every `WhoRow`, so this was purely a rendering change — no data-flow changes needed.

## Verification

- All 8 `who_tests` pass (updated their assertions to expect the host).
- Confirmed against the live daemon — every agent shows `(laptop)`, the remote shows `(tenex kind2, remote)`.

## Notes on the working tree

- I discovered `src/cli/who.rs` and its submodules are **dead code** — they're never declared with `mod who;` anywhere, so the live implementation is the inline copy in `src/cli.rs`. I edited the dead files first by mistake and reverted them, keeping the diff surgical. (This duplicate-copy situation is a latent footgun worth cleaning up separately — commit `bd91c352` already hit confusion over "the correct WhoSnapshot".)
- I added a missing `attachable: false` to two test literals in the same `who_tests` module — another concurrent session added that struct field but left these literals incomplete, which was blocking test compilation.
